memory.summary: I have a free afternoon, but the term project is due Thursday and barely started. I need to resist the urge to choose leisure now and prioritize the coursework block instead.

desires: Resist the immediate urge to hang out with cohort_3 so I can actually start the term project before Thursday.
Avoid looking like I'm coasting on my teammates' work once we sync up next week.
Not crash into a deep sleep deficit by 2 AM tonight despite having 'free time'.

goals.description: Execute the 'quick_chore' immediately to clear my mental stack before attempting any term project work, ensuring I don't procrastinate on high-value tasks by hiding in low-stakes leisure.
goals.rationale: As an Affiliation/Assertive/Deliberate persona, I value long-term payoff over immediate comfort. Starting the term project is crucial for our group's future success (Affiliation), but my current energy and focus are compromised by sleep debt. The 'chore' is a deliberate, low-friction step to reset my state without the social friction of hanging out with cohort_3 or the anxiety of diving straight into complex work while tired.

goals.description: Draft a firm boundary message to cohort_3 declining their invitation for this afternoon, citing the need to protect deep work time for the team project.
goals.rationale: My assertiveness score is 8, so setting boundaries is non-negotiable. However, doing this while my brain is foggy from sleep debt might make me sound weak rather than confident. I'll defer this until after the chore gives me a small dopamine hit and a moment of clarity.

agent.last_reasoning: I must clear this trivial win first to reset my mental stack before tackling the term project. Skipping it risks hiding in leisure, which violates my long-term planning horizon. I'll do this now so I can later message cohort_3 with clarity rather than fatigue.
